title: Parenting System for Limiting Screen Time
description: Parents don’t struggle because kids love screens, every kid does. The real problem is when screens quietly become the family’s default babysitter. Inside, you’ll find a full 5-step system to keep screens in check without turning into the “Bad Cop,” plus bite-sized resets for marriage connection, family rituals, and your own mental health guilt spirals.
author: PowerPair

title: Stop Flying Blind: The Family Vision & Goals System That Actually Works
description: Running a family without shared values is like trying to GPS somewhere without knowing the destination. Inside: the bite-sized tactics that make daily life smoother, plus a 4-step system to help your family stop drifting and start building something intentional together.
author: PowerPair

title: Marriage Autopilot Check + How to Weather a Storm
description: Three bite-sized tactics for marriage, parenting, and mental health. Plus a family system that creates connection without the chaos. For parents who want real solutions, not Instagram-perfect fantasies.
author: PowerPair

title: Screenless Dinners + 10-Min Reading System
description: Three bite-sized tactics for marriage, parenting, and mental health. Plus a family system that creates connection without the chaos. For parents who want real solutions, not Instagram-perfect fantasies.
author: PowerPair

title: Burnout-Proof Your Evenings: 10-Min Transition Framework for Working Parents
description: Here's your systematic approach for busy families to shift from work stress to family presence without carrying office chaos into your most important relationships.
author: PowerPair

title: The Burnout Cure Isn't Sleep: The 7 Types of Rest for Busy Parents
description: Here's your systematic approach to identify what kind of exhausted you actually are and get the targeted rest that truly restores your energy.
author: PowerPair

title: Quality Over Quantity Time with Kids: Working Parent's Presence Framework
description: Here's your framework for working parents to be truly present and build deeper connections with their kids when time is limited.
author: PowerPair
